title: Aspect ratio film
summary: The aspect ratio of an image is the ratio of its width to its height. It is expressed as two numbers separated by a colon, in the format width:height. Common aspect ratios are 1.85:1 and 2.39:1 in cinematography , 4:3 (originally) and 16:9 in television , and 3:2 in still photography . Other common display aspect ratios include 5:4 and 16:10.
